15 outbreaks (12 in poultry, 3 in other captive birds)

## Type of establishments:

- 5 Hobby holdings (mixed breeds incl. Pigeons, ducks and hens and other ornamental birds).
- 5 Layers
- 4 Turkeys for slaughter
- 2 Pheasants

<!-- image -->

## 2025/2026 HPAI H5N1 in Denmark Since last presentation at SCoPAFF in November 2025

7 new outbreaks in poultry and captive birds since the 22nd of November 2025

## Outbreaks handled according to Commission delegated regulation 2020/687

- 3 and 10 km zones established
- 3 hobby establishments categorized as other captive birds <50 birds, no RZ
- Culling and disposal of animals completed
- Cleaning and disinfection

## Outbreak 11: Hens + pigeons Clinical signs in hens (7)

- Pigeons kept in separate enclosure, no clinical signs
- -Positive cloacal swabs
- -Kadavers sent for further analysis, awaiting results

<!-- image -->

## Epidemiologal investigations Source of infection

## Control of biosecurity measures

- Control of possible breaches/ entry of virus e.g. ventilation shafts, storage of food, insufficient barriers, lack of compliance with restrictions (under roofing/indoor housing) etc.
- Preliminary investigations suggest inddirect contact with wild birds
- Only primary outbreaks, based on epidemiological investigations
- -Epidemiological assessments are ongoing (Danish Veterinary Consortium)
- Outbreak 1-14 and wild birds from same time period: Genotype DI.2.1
- -No signs of mutations/adaptations to mammals
- -Awaiting results from outbreak 15

- ·
- National regulation ('housing order')

(Result of rapid risk assessment Oktober 2025)

- Poultry and captive birds under roof/indoor housing
- -Ducks and geese: require implementation of further biosecurity measures in order to keep them outdoor, keep wild birds away from the enclosure
- Ducks and geese should be kept apart/no contact with other poultry
- Feed and water under roof/indoor
- Watering holes etc. must be sheltered from wild birds
- Ban of markets, exhibitions and other gatherings

<!-- image -->

## High risk of HPAI, enforcement of restrictions

Highly pathogenic avian influenza in Europe in relation to the threat to Denmark per 23 October, 2025

## Rapid risk assessment:

The Danish Veterinary and Food Administration has assessed the risk of highly pathogenic avian influenza (HPA1), which replaces the assessment from 8 October, 2025.

The Danish Veterinary and Food Administration estimates that the overall probability of introduction for HPAI to Danish poultry herds due to contact with wild birds increasesfrom MEDIUM toHIGH.

The Danish Veterinary and Food Administration also assesses that the overall risk of infection with HPAI to Danish poultry through trade is unchanged and remains low.

| Risk pathway                       | Probability of introduction level 23/10-2025   |
|------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------|
| Wild birds                         | High                                           |
| Trade of poultry and hatching eggs | Low                                            |

## Risk mitigation:

As a consequence of the increased probability of introduction, the Danish Veterinary and Food Administration wants to draw your attention to:

1. The probability of introduction level has increased, as the number of outbreaks in western Europe is rising.
2. Based on the increasing probability of introduction to high', requirements for 3. confinement and covering of poultry will be introduced from 27 October 2025.
3. HPAI has been detected in different species of wild birds in several places in 2. Denmark, suggesting widespread infection in wild birds.
4. Poultry and bird holders are recommended to maintain very high levels of biosecurity on top of the obligations above. Biosecurity is the responsibility of the operator.
5. The Danish Veterinary and Food Administration encourages the public to re5. port findings of dead wild birds.

The Danish Veterinary and Food Administration closely monitors the situation in Europe and updates the assessment, when it is considered relevant.

## Further preventive actions- awareness

- Weekly expert group meetings
- Press relases and materials on webpage, SoMe
- Meetings with organisations, industry
- Collaboration with journalists/national media

## Vaccination strategy in confined

## etsablishments in Denmark SCoPAFF-meeting 22.-23. January 2026

## HPAI vaccination strategy in confined establishments in Denmark 2026

## Background for vaccination strategy

- Kept birds in risk of HPAI-infection despite biosecurity measures
- Endangered species - conservational purposes - birds with long life-span - breeding programmes

## Vaccination strategy

- Preventive vaccination in compliance with CDR (EU) 2023/361 article 7(1)(b)
- Requirements in compliance with Annex XIII, Part V of CDR (EU) 2023/361:
- Passive and active surveillance implemented by establishments
- Vaccine used is Nobilis Influenza H5N2 (MSD) (off-label)
- DVFAFA perform control every 30 days
- Vaccine obtained from Danish Medicines Agency only by veterinarians with individual permission issued by DVFAFA
- Movements of birds from vaccinated establishments - only with permission in accordance with DR (2023/361, annex XIII, part 5.4)

<!-- image -->

A man in a shirt and glasses looks at some green plants in front of a glass wall.

<!-- image -->

## HPAI vaccination strategy in confined establishments in Denmark 2026

- Records in establishments
- Species, no. of vaccinated birds (incl. indidual ID),no. of doses administered, date of vaccination
- Annual reporting
- collected by DVFAFA. Information to be shared with European Commission
- Vaccination permits granted for 2026
- 6 confined establishments - all zoological gardens
- potential no of birds 988 total
